notes: Center for Internet Security is a non-profit operating the MS-ISAC and EI-ISAC, publishing the CIS Benchmarks and CIS Controls, and maintaining OVAL (transferred from MITRE in 2017). The control-type entry control/org/cisecurity.json covers normative CIS Controls; this reference-type entry covers other CIS-maintained specifications and reference data such as OVAL.

data.description: International, open standard for the structured assessment and reporting of machine state — used to describe how to check for vulnerabilities, configuration issues, patches, and inventoried software on a system.
data.notes: OVAL definitions are XML documents that name a test and describe how to evaluate it across supported platforms. Originally a MITRE project (with US-CERT/DHS sponsorship); maintenance transferred to CIS Center for Internet Security in 2017. The OVAL Repository and the OVAL Language specification continue at CIS.

examples.note: The OVAL language specification and repository
